meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to leverage a client-first workflow to identify high-revenue SaaS opportunities using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start hunting for 'pain'. By implementing a structured client workflow, you analyze existing business inefficiencies to build profitable SaaS solutions. Use Antigravity to map these gaps and Claude Code to accelerate your MVP development, ensuring every line of code solves a specific, revenue-generating business problem.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re sitting there with the technical stack ready, the drive to execute, but no clear target to hit. Most founders fail here because they look for 'cool apps' instead of 'expensive problems.' The secret isn't a flash of genius; it’s a systematic audit of boring, manual business workflows that bleed money every single day.

We are shifting your focus from 'what can I build' to 'what is someone already paying a human to do manually.' By treating business operations as an engineering problem, you stop building toys and start building assets. This guide provides the tactical roadmap to turn observation into a deployable SaaS MVP using modern AI-driven development tools.

steps.title: Identify a Manual Bottleneck
steps.description: Find a business process that relies on spreadsheets, email, or manual data entry. If a business owner is paying a salary for a repetitive task, that is your beachhead market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business process description: [Insert Process]. Identify the top 3 manual bottlenecks that cause the most time loss and suggest a SaaS architecture that automates these specific tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a project outline for a SaaS tool that automates [Bottleneck] for [Target Industry].'

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip away the 'nice-to-haves.' Your MVP only needs to solve one specific pain point well enough to justify a monthly subscription.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal feature set for a SaaS MVP targeting [Target Industry]. Prioritize features based on highest impact to revenue and lowest development complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project structure for a Node.js/React app focusing only on [Core Feature].'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: A clean data model is the backbone of your application. Ensure your schema supports the specific workflow you identified in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS app that tracks [Workflow]. Include entities for users, client data, and the automation state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create Prisma schema file based on [Schema Design] and include migration scripts.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the business logic that handles your client's unique workflow requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic in TypeScript that processes [Input Data] and transforms it into [Desired Output] for my SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the main service layer for the [Workflow] automation logic.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Get your MVP into the hands of a real user immediately. Feedback is the only metric that matters at the beginner stage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted SaaS app, including environment variables, database connection strings, and security best practices.

pro_tips: Always build for the 'unsexy' industries—logistics, accounting, or legal—where businesses have high churn but high budget.
Do not build a landing page until your MVP can perform the core task in a CLI or demo environment; validate the utility before the marketing.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests simultaneously with the feature code to prevent technical debt from slowing your MVP iteration.
Charge from day one. Even a $10/month subscription validates the problem more than a thousand free users.
If you find yourself adding a feature that doesn't save the client time or money, delete it immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, find five potential users in your target niche. Offer them free access in exchange for a 15-minute feedback call to identify the next feature to build.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the perfect solution. Your first paying customer is waiting for you to solve their biggest headache. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
